Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

(1 comment)

Patch Set 3:

before: 64bytes. after: 40 bytes

Patch Set 3:

Code-wise looks good to me.

Patch Set 3:

PPC64 Builder: build succesful

Patch Set 3:

Hm, 64->40 bytes allocation is a noop, is not it?
Kernel rounds up the allocation to the next power of two I think?

Patch Set 3:

Newer kernels have slab sizes that are 2^n*3/4, like 48, 96, 192 bytes. Even so, better to reduce memory usage of possible so that if it grows again in the future it won't take even more space.

Patch Set 3:

(14 comments)

White space improvements possible of patch needs to be refreshed. 

Oleg, the lov_io->lis_subs array allocates these structs in an array, so any space savings helps there.
Patch Set 3: Code-Review+2
Change has been successfully cherry-picked as e1e56300cac30fe8d9db296107905f5936648c3c

commit.subject: LU-7085 lov: trying smaller memory allocations
commit.message:
LU-7085 lov: trying smaller memory allocations

Reduce struct lov_io_sub to smaller memory usage
on wide-stripe file systems.

Signed-off-by: Yang Sheng <yang.sheng@intel.com>
Change-Id: Ie92cec079f6514efca71663af1ae6ae1e34c73cb
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/17476
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Bob Glossman <bob.glossman@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Jian Yu <jian.yu@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
